source: All-In Podcast — E274: SpaceX $2T case, Nvidia shock selloff
summary: SpaceX's terrestrial data center business alone (Colossus 3, 4, etc.) could reach $100-200B in revenue by 2030-2032, justifying the $2T valuation on that segment alone
summary_extended: Baker (alongside Chamath) argues that SpaceX's terrestrial data center business alone could generate $100–200B in annual revenue by 2030–2032, potentially justifying the company's $2T valuation on that segment in isolation. The core reasoning is that gigawatt-scale data center build capability is extraordinarily rare, and SpaceX is uniquely positioned as the closest entity to delivering an actual gigawatt-scale facility — exemplified by Colossus — making it highly attractive to hyperscale AI customers like Anthropic. At a ~$2T valuation, investors are paying roughly 20x 2026 estimated revenue of ~$100B+ across all SpaceX segments, but the terrestrial data center business alone may grow to justify that multiple. The investment implication centers on SpaceX's private market valuation, as the company is not publicly listed; no directly tradeable US-listed equity is the clear subject or unambiguous beneficiary of this specific thesis. Baker's own contribution is primarily additive color on Jensen/NVIDIA GPU allocation dynamics and SpaceX's speed advantage in data center construction, rather than an explicit endorsement of the $100–200B revenue figure.
detail: Chamath's underwriting case (endorsed by Baker's presence/agreement): SpaceX at $2T is being bought at ~20x 2026E revenue of ~$100B+ (terrestrial data centers alone). The gigawatt-scale data center build capability is extremely rare and valuable. Colossus's value to Anthropic stems from SpaceX being closest to delivering an actual gigawatt data center. Terrestrial data centers alone = $100-200B revenue by 2030-2032.
confidence_evidence: This is primarily Chamath's analysis; Baker adds color on Jensen GPU allocation dynamics and SpaceX's data center speed advantage but does not explicitly endorse the $100-200B figure
quote_fragment: terrestrial data centers alone are 100 or 200 billion dollars of revenue by 2030
